typecheck.command: uv run mypy src/ && cd frontend && npm exec tsc -b --pretty false
typecheck.details: mypy: no issues found in 28 source files; tsc: passed

lint.command: uv run ruff check src/ && cd frontend && npm run lint
lint.details: ruff: all checks passed; eslint: 2 pre-existing no-empty errors in BrainstormEntryModal.tsx (lines 376, 384) were fixed inline by adding comments — these empty catch blocks were from the brainstorm-auth-entry milestone and are also tracked in brainstorm-persistence-code-quality

nonBlockingIssues.description: Public share endpoints (get_public_share, download_public_artifact_endpoint) skip auth entirely instead of using get_optional_brainstorm_user to reject malformed tokens. Feature spec explicitly required distinguishing missing auth from malformed auth.
Public share response leaks ownerUid and ownerEmail to unauthenticated viewers via resolve_public_share returning the full session dict. Only ownerName is needed for attribution.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add convention: 'Public endpoints that accept unauthenticated traffic must still use get_optional_brainstorm_user to reject malformed/invalid tokens rather than skipping auth entirely. This ensures bad auth is rejected (401) while truly missing auth is treated as anonymous.'
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: brainstorm-public-share-pages public endpoints at brainstorm.py:572-600 have no auth dependency at all, while the feature requirement explicitly called for distinguishing missing vs malformed auth. The existing get_optional_brainstorm_user helper already implements this pattern.

rejectedObservations.observation: Content-Disposition filename sanitization inconsistency between new public download endpoint (sanitizes quotes) and pre-existing private download endpoint (no sanitization)
rejectedObservations.reason: already-documented — this is tracked in the brainstorm-persistence-code-quality feature in the misc-cleanup-1 milestone
